title: Careful-flow MCP wrappers + project/spec discovery MCP
description: Two coordinated additions for cockpit sidebar functionality. (a) Careful-flow wrappers: bober_list_pending_approvals, bober_approve_checkpoint, bober_reject_checkpoint — thin MCP adapters over src/state/approval-state.ts and the existing CLI logic in src/cli/commands/{approve,reject,list-approvals}.ts. (b) Discovery: bober_list_projects({ searchRoots }), bober_list_specs({ projectPath }), bober_get_project_state({ projectPath }) — enable the cockpit's project-list sidebar by walking one level under each searchRoot for bober.config.json, enumerating .bober/specs/, and aggregating per-project counts (active runs, open incidents, pending approvals).

successCriteria.description: src/mcp/tools/list-pending-approvals.ts registers bober_list_pending_approvals; accepts optional `{ projectPath?: string }`; returns the same array shape as `bober list-approvals --json` (array of `{ checkpointId, ageMs, prompt }`).

src/mcp/tools/approve-checkpoint.ts registers bober_approve_checkpoint; accepts `{ checkpointId, projectPath?, editDelta? }`; writes .bober/approvals/<id>.approved.json with the same payload shape as the CLI; returns `{ approvedAt, checkpointId }` JSON.

src/mcp/tools/reject-checkpoint.ts registers bober_reject_checkpoint; accepts `{ checkpointId, projectPath?, feedback }`; feedback required and non-empty; writes .bober/approvals/<id>.rejected.json with `{ rejectedAt, rejecterId, feedback }`; returns `{ rejectedAt, checkpointId }` JSON.

src/mcp/tools/list-projects.ts registers bober_list_projects; accepts `{ searchRoots: string[] }`; walks each root one level deep; returns `[{ projectPath, name, mode, hasActiveRuns, lastRunAt? }]` for every directory containing bober.config.json.
src/mcp/tools/list-specs.ts registers bober_list_specs; accepts `{ projectPath }`; reads .bober/specs/*.json; returns `[{ specId, title, status, sprintCount, completedAt? }]`.

src/mcp/tools/get-project-state.ts registers bober_get_project_state; accepts `{ projectPath }`; returns `{ configExists, activeRunCount, lastRunAt?, openIncidentCount, pendingApprovalCount, specCount, mode? }`.
All six tools reject relative paths in the projectPath argument with `{ error: 'projectPath must be absolute' }` JSON; verified by negative test.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, and `npm run test` all pass.

nonGoals: Do not modify the existing CLI subcommands (approve, reject, list-approvals); the MCP tools and CLI live side-by-side and share the helpers in src/state/approval-state.ts.
Do not implement caching for list-projects results — every call walks the filesystem fresh. Performance is acceptable for cockpit-scale (tens of projects).
Do not implement deep-recursive project discovery — one level deep under each searchRoot is the documented contract.
Do not expose write access to .bober/specs/ via MCP — list-only.
Do not introduce new npm dependencies.
stopConditions: All required success criteria pass evaluation.

Integration smoke: create a fixture project with one pending approval, invoke bober_list_pending_approvals, approve it via bober_approve_checkpoint, verify the .approved.json marker file appears with the correct payload shape.
Cross-tool smoke: bober_get_project_state on a project with 2 specs, 1 active run, 0 incidents, 1 pending approval returns the correct counts.
definitionOfDone: The cockpit can drive the careful-flow approval lifecycle over MCP without shelling out to the bober CLI: list pending approvals across one or more projects, approve or reject each by checkpointId. The cockpit can also render a sidebar of all known projects under a set of user-configured search roots, listing each project's specs and aggregated state (counts of active runs, open incidents, pending approvals). All six tools follow the existing MCP pattern (registerTool, zod-friendly inputSchema, JSON.stringify return, JSON soft errors, absolute-path validation).
assumptions: src/state/approval-state.ts already exposes pendingExists() — generator should also export a listPendingApprovals(projectRoot) helper used by both the CLI and the new MCP tool. Refactor the CLI command to use the new helper for consistency.

Incident discovery: src/incident/timeline.ts:listIncidents already exists and returns IncidentSummary[]. Reuse it; openIncidentCount = incidents.filter(i => i.status !== 'resolved' && i.status !== 'aborted').length.

Active run count from RunManager: use the Sprint 1 listActiveRuns().filter(r => r.status === 'running').length; per-project, but each MCP server instance owns one projectRoot — so when called against a different projectPath, the count comes from .bober/runs/*/state.json on that path (load on-demand, do not require pre-population).
Project name in list-projects: read bober.config.json's project.name field; fall back to basename(projectPath) if missing.
lastRunAt: max(startedAt) across all .bober/runs/*/state.json files in the project.
outOfScope: Write access to specs/contracts via MCP (read-only sufficient for the cockpit sidebar).
Permission/auth checks on cross-project queries — cockpit's job.
Cross-project event aggregation (each MCP server is per-project).
generatorNotes: PATTERNS TO FOLLOW: src/cli/commands/approve.ts (read full file) for the approval payload shape and pendingExists() guard; src/cli/commands/reject.ts for the rejection payload; src/cli/commands/list-approvals.ts for the pending-list reader. EXTRACT a new helper listPendingApprovals(projectRoot) in src/state/approval-state.ts so both CLI and MCP share it. PATTERN for the registerTool template: src/mcp/tools/run.ts and src/mcp/tools/status.ts. CRITICAL: All six tools accept an optional projectPath; when omitted, default to cwd(). When provided, MUST be absolute (validate via path.isAbsolute()); reject relative with `{ error: 'projectPath must be absolute' }` JSON soft error. The discovery tools (list-projects, list-specs, get-project-state) are READ-ONLY on disk — they do NOT instantiate RunManager. To get activeRunCount for an arbitrary projectPath, write a standalone helper readRunStatesFromDisk(projectRoot) in src/state/run-state.ts (mirrors the Sprint 1 module's read-side). For list-specs, read .bober/specs/*.json and parse via the existing PlanSpecSchema (loose-parse — tolerate version mismatches). Sprint mode field for the project: read bober.config.json's project.mode if present (greenfield/brownfield).
evaluatorNotes: Verify all six tools follow the existing registerTool + JSON.stringify pattern exactly. Verify the absolute-path validation works — pass './foo' as projectPath and assert the soft-error JSON is returned. Verify the careful-flow tools share state with the CLI commands by exercising a mixed-mode scenario: write a pending approval, list it via bober_list_pending_approvals, approve via bober_approve_checkpoint, then use the CLI `bober list-approvals` to confirm it's gone. Verify list-projects returns ONLY directories containing bober.config.json; directories without it are skipped. Verify list-specs returns the correct title/status/sprintCount for each .bober/specs/<id>.json. Verify get-project-state correctly sums activeRunCount across .bober/runs/*/state.json. Negative test: list-projects with an unreadable searchRoot returns a soft error or skips that root with a warning to stderr, doesn't throw.
